sentence: The great-grandfather of Petyr Baelish was a Braavosi sellsword who came to the Vale of Arryn in service to House Corbray.
Balerion, called the Black Dread, was the dragon ridden by King Aegon I Targaryen during the Conquest.
An old black tomcat with a torn ear, believed to be the kitten once owned by Princess Rhaenys Targaryen.
The brewer at Winterfell, whose wife took Theon Greyjoy's virginity.
A King in the North of House Stark who is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.
A King in the North of House Stark who is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.
A girl from Hag's Mire who was murdered by Chett after rejecting and mocking him.
A tavern wench who works at the Smoking Log in the winter town outside Winterfell.
A red stallion with a temper to match his master's, owned by Ramsay Bolton.
A young thrall and oarsman in the Iron Fleet who has been rowing for less than a year and is roughly twenty years old.
Brandon the Bad was a King in the North of House Stark who is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.
A King of Winter who formed an alliance with Joramun to end the thirteen-year rule of the Night's King at the Wall.
A King in the North known for his love of the sea who attempted to sail across the Sunset Sea thousands of years before Aegon's Conquest and was never seen again.
Cleon is a butcher and cook enslaved to Grazdan mo Ullhor.
Dagon Greyjoy, called Dagon the Drunkard, is a cousin of Asha Greyjoy.
Dalla is a serving woman at Dragonstone.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran Bronze-Axe, was a Storm King of House Durrandon.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Dour, was a Storm King who slew Lun the Last, King of the Giants.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Fair, was a Storm King who brought Tarth into his kingdom.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Ravenfriend, was a Storm King of House Durrandon.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Third, was a Storm King who attempted invasions of Dorne.
Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Young and the Butcher Boy, was a Storm King.
Erich Durrandon, also known as Erich Kin-Killer, was a member of House Durrandon.
Gerold Grafton was an Andal knight from Andalos who established House Grafton over Gulltown.
Gerold Lannister, called Gerold the Great, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister.
Grazdan is a Good Master of Astapor.
A Good Master of Astapor with a distinctive spiked beard who negotiates the sale of Unsullied to Daenerys.
A stablehand from Mole's Town who sought to join the Night's Watch and was trained as a recruit at Castle Black.
A stableman in the town of Ashford during Daeron II's reign who purchased the horse Sweetfoot from Ser Duncan the Tall.
An Unsullied warrior who served as second-in-command of Daenerys's Unsullied forces after Grey Worm.
A dog that belonged to Podrick Payne when he was young; mentioned as a good dog in a brief childhood memory.
A dog owned by Ramsay Bolton, one of the bastard's girls.
A serving girl at Winterfell who was replaced by spearwives disguised as servants after Ramsay Bolton's wedding.
An ancient pirate-king of House Massey who fought a war over Massey's Hook against Storm King Erich VII Durrandon during the Andal invasion.
A man from Flea Bottom who fought for Stannis Baratheon at the Battle of the Blackwater, where he was killed on the bridge of ships.
A fourteen-year-old prostitute at the Happy Port brothel in Braavos with long golden hair and high market value.
A serving wench and prostitute at the Peach inn in Stoney Sept in the Riverlands.
A peasant of House Osgrey who survived the Blackfyre Rebellion and was called to defend his lands against House Webber.
Lord of Rook's Rest during the reigns of Kings Maegor I and Jaehaerys I, he retained loyalty after Maegor's death and later died with his family of the Shivers in 59 AC.
High King of the Iron Islands known as the Old Kraken, he led an invasion of the North and retook Bear Island and Cape Kraken before his death.
Blacksmith and master of the armory at Harrenhal, beheaded by Steelshanks Walton for making weapons for the Lannisters during the fall of Harrenhal in 299 AC.
A recruit of the Night's Watch in Jon Snow's class, he was trained as a ranger and remained stationed at Castle Black.
A Dothraki khal and sometime ally of Drogo, father of Rhogoro, he attended Daenerys's presentation in Pentos.
Son of the miller of Donnelwood, he was slain by House Lannister forces during the War of the Five Kings.
A stoop-backed old swineherd in service to House Osgrey during King Aerys I's reign, he led devotions in the sept and was a follower of the Faith of the Seven.
A member of Yoren's band marching to the Wall for the Night's Watch, his status as recruit, criminal, or orphan is unknown; his fate after the band's destruction is unrecorded.
One of the best huntsmen in service to House Stark, he was ordered by Theon Greyjoy to accompany him in searching for Bran and Rickon Stark.
Squire to Ser Desmond Grell, the master-at-arms of Riverrun, he was slain by Jaime Lannister during a rescue attempt in 299 AC.
A dragonseed from Driftmark who tamed the wild dragon Sheepstealer during the Dance of the Dragons and fought valiantly for the Blacks before escaping with Daemon Targaryen.
A spearwife of the free folk under Iron Emmett's command at Long Barrow.
A direwolf bonded to Arya Stark, named after Princess Nymeria of the Rhoynar.
A King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare who promoted the Faith of the Seven, discouraged reaving, and encouraged trade, opposed by Drowned God priests.
A King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare who promoted the Faith of the Seven, discouraged reaving, and encouraged trade, opposed by Drowned God priests.
A riverman who fought in the War of the Ninepenny Kings and died in 260 AC from a mace blow at the Stepstones.
A large Valeman knight of the Vale who competed in the Gates of the Moon tourney for the Brotherhood of Winged Knights.
A Citadel novice from the westerlands, POV of AFFC prologue, poisoned by the alchemist Jaqen H'ghar over a stolen Citadel key.
A boy orphaned by the War of the Five Kings, taken in by the Heddles at the crossroads inn.
A peasant sworn to House Osgrey who survived the Blackfyre Rebellion and had weepy eyes, snored, and liked to talk of camp followers.
A Blackwood nobleman who participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, possibly a second son with personal arms of two weirwood trees.
A riverman, brother of Septon Meribald, who died from fever during the War of the Ninepenny Kings at the Stepstones in 260 AC.
A candlemaker's daughter from Flea Bottom during the Dance of the Dragons who, by legend, comforted Prince Joffrey Velaryon as he died.
An ancient King in the North buried at Winterfell, reportedly won Bear Island from the ironborn and granted it to House Mormont.
Rosey is the daughter of Emma, a serving wench at the Quill and Tankard in Oldtown.
Simon Staunton was a squire from House Staunton during the reign of King Jaehaerys I Targaryen.
Sly is a female wolf. She is one of Varamyr's pack, each of which he can enter as a warg. She is cunning.
Sly was a stableboy at Hogs Head, a disreputable inn in Bitterbridge, during the Dance of the Dragons.
Tansy is an innkeep at the Peach, a brothel in Stoney Sept.
Tansy is a girl orphaned by the War of the Five Kings who is currently living at the crossroads inn.
Timett is the father of Timett of the Burned Men in the Vale of Arryn.
Torrhen Stark, known as the King Who Knelt, was a head of House Stark who reigned as King in the North until Aegon I Targaryen invaded Westeros with his dragons.
Tybolt Lannister, dubbed the Thunderbolt, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock.
The third Vulture King was a Dornish rebel active during the Dance of the Dragons.
The Vulture King was a Dornish outlaw who gathered thousands of followers to rise against King Aenys I Targaryen in the Second Dornish War. His given name is unknown.
The Vulture King was a self-proclaimed king who rose up against the Iron Throne from the Red Mountains of Dorne during the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen.
The second Vulture King was a self-proclaimed Dornish king who raided in the Third Dornish War during the reign of King Jaehaerys I Targaryen.
Known as Walton the Moon King, he was a King in the North and is buried in Winterfell's crypt.
Brother of Jack-Be-Lucky, sent to the Wall to serve in the Night's Watch.
An orphan from the War of the Five Kings taken in by the Heddles at the Inn at the crossroads.
A crew member of the Brazen Monkey who approached Cat of the Canals in Braavos.
A peasant of House Osgrey in the Reach during Aerys I's reign, trained by Duncan the Tall for a war over the Chequy Water.
An orphan boy taken in by the Heddles at the crossroads inn alongside Wat and other War of the Five Kings orphans.
A gawky squire of Ser Uthor Underleaf during Aerys I's reign, born around 192 AC.
